response:
Here are pytest-style unit tests covering happy paths, edge cases, and error cases. No special formatting used.

```python
import pytest
from your_module import fizzbuzz

def test_fizzbuzz_returns_fizzbuzz_for_multiples_of_15():
    assert fizzbuzz(15) == "FizzBuzz"
    assert fizzbuzz(30) == "FizzBuzz"

def test_fizzbuzz_returns_fizz_for_multiples_of_3_only():
    assert fizzbuzz(3) == "Fizz"
    assert fizzbuzz(9) == "Fizz"

def test_fizzbuzz_returns_buzz_for_multiples_of_5_only():
    assert fizzbuzz(5) == "Buzz"
    assert fizzbuzz(20) == "Buzz"

def test_fizzbuzz_returns_number_string_when_not_multiple_of_3_or_5():
    assert fizzbuzz(1) == "1"
    assert fizzbuzz(7) == "7"

def test_fizzbuzz_edge_case_zero():
    # 0 is divisible by any nonzero integer, so treat as FizzBuzz
    assert fizzbuzz(0) == "FizzBuzz"

def test_fizzbuzz_negative_numbers():
    assert fizzbuzz(-3) == "Fizz"
    assert fizzbuzz(-5) == "Buzz"
    assert fizzbuzz(-15) == "FizzBuzz"
    assert fizzbuzz(-1) == "-1"

def test_fizzbuzz_invalid_input_type():
    with pytest.raises(TypeError):
        fizzbuzz("abc")

    with pytest.raises(TypeError):
        fizzbuzz(None)

    with pytest.raises(TypeError):
        fizzbuzz(3.5)
```

If needed, I can add parameterized tests or additional edge cases.
